Erikkila – Smart solutions in Light Crane technology
Erikkila innovates, manufactures, sells, and services light cranes, jib cranes, and portal cranes for industrial applications.
As part of Kito Crosby, we are committed to delivering high-quality, safe, and user-friendly products that meet strict environmental standards. Our core values—safety, quality, excellent customer service, and fast delivery—guide everything we do.
Why choose Erikkila?
Our cranes are renowned for their exceptional build quality, easy customization, and comprehensive factory support, including rapid spare part availability and maintenance services. With a global network of experienced partners, you can count on expert support wherever you are.

ERIKKILA OY

Our high-quality, user-friendly solutions meet modern environmental standards and deliver reliable performance. With fast delivery and excellent customer service, ERIKKILA supports efficient industrial lifting worldwide. Proudly part of the Kito Crosby Group.

about.text:
About — Erikkila
About us
About ERIKKILA
History
Career at ERIKKILA
References
Contact us
Erikkila has 50 years of experience in light lifting solutions and is part of Columbus McKinnon.
100 years of history
Key milestones and achievements.
1912
Erikkilä was founded in Vyborg, the centre of international trade in Finland.
1917
Europe and Finland in turmoil, the company moved to northwest Finland, to Raahe.
1933
The company moved to Helsinki and signed its first crane representation agreement with the German Heinrich de Fries (HADEF).
1976
The company concentrated solely on lifting equipment.
1977-1988
Technical innovations and crane development were at the core of the operations. The company innovated, designed, sold and installed, while crane manufacturing was outsourced.
1977
PROSYSTEM product innovation and the first company in Scandinavia manufacturing PROSYSTEM light crane systems.
1978
First in the world to develop a raised bridge construction to light crane systems.
1987
The official name of the company was changed to Erikkilä Nostotekniikkaa Oy.
1988
The company started its own PROSYSTEM light crane systems production in Espoo.
1989
The first manufacturer of light crane systems to use powder painting of crane profiles. First in patenting ROBSYSTEM robotic cranes.
1991
First western crane company establishing a factory in Estonia.
1995
The growth of the company required more extensive facilities. The factory and office moved to Masala, Kirkkonummi, to its present location.
1996
First to patent integrated rubber buffers in PROSYSTEM push trolley.
2003
First Finnish crane manufacturer to launch a light crane system made of aluminium.
2004
Launch of a new generation PROSYSTEM Light Crane System to the market. First to invent and patent an overload indicator to PROSYSTEM light crane systems making it one of the safest in the world. Also, first to pack crane components in a vacuum.
2005
Member of FEM (Federation of European Material handling Manufacturers) organisation. Active participation in developing the first European Light Crane Standard and serving as the chairman of the standardisation group.
2010 - 2011
The company name changed to ERIKKILA Oy. Expansion of the production facilities in Masala to a total size of 3500 m
2012
Publication of a book on ERIKKILA's 100-year history.
2018
Kito Corporation acquires all ERIKKILA Oy shares in Finland through its' subsidiary Kito Europe GmbH, making ERIKKILA part of the KITO Group in Europe.
2019
Knut Stewen is appointed as the new managing director.
2023
The Crosby Group and KITO CORPORATION complete business combination and Kito Crosby is formed.

* Our e-invoice address is the same as our OVT code (party identification number). The Finnish OVT code consists of the country code "0037" + the business ID without the hyphen + an optional department/unit ID (max. 5 characters).
Please use the above e-invoice address and intermediary's ID for invoice routing. If you are sending e-invoices via Tieto (formerly TietoEnator), please add the letters "AN" in front of the e-invoice address; i.e. the address should read "AN003706735630".
If sending paper-based purchase invoices, please send them to the following address:
